data.title: Agent Kudos System: User-awarded points
data.description:
Placeholder epic for human-adjudicated kudos awards. Deferred from epic 3e9b-afee (Agent Kudos System core) to keep V1 scope manageable.

RESEARCH NOTES FROM BRAINSTORM SESSION (2026-04-08):

MOTIVATION: V1 kudos system is fully mechanical (no LLM judgment, no human input). User-awarded points would add a human oversight layer and cover quality behaviors that are observable to humans but not structurally verifiable from git diff alone.

CANDIDATE TRIGGER PATTERNS:
- User explicitly recognizes a design decision during interactive session ('that was a great approach')
- User approves a particularly well-reasoned implementation-plan proposal
- User notes that an explanation or doc update was especially clear
- Post-session retro: human rates session quality and awards bonus points

DESIGN CONSIDERATIONS:
- Mechanism: CLI command or slash command for human to award (e.g., /dso:kudos award <points> <reason>)
- Integrity: human-awarded points should be stored separately from mechanical awards in .kudos/ ledger (different trigger_id prefix) to maintain audit trail
- Anti-gaming: human-awarded points should have a session cap independent of mechanical cap to prevent solicitation behavior
- Solicitation disqualifier: agent explicitly asking for kudos points should disqualify the session from human awards
- Goodhart risk: higher risk than mechanical triggers since human judgment is more gameable (agents could optimize for human approval rather than quality)
- Attribution: human-awarded points carry session ID of the human session, not agent session

INTEGRATION QUESTIONS:
- Should human awards propagate to sub-agent framing in subsequent sessions?
- Slack/comment integration: award via PR comment or Slack reaction?
- Cross-session visibility: should human awards be visible in the running total display?

OPEN RESEARCH:
- Social recognition systems in software teams (peer recognition vs. manager recognition)
- Gamification research on human-in-the-loop reward systems
- Whether human awards reinforce or undermine mechanical award signal
